title: caladium bicolor var. houlletii
intro: Caladium bicolor var. houlletii, also known as Houllet's caladium or Elephant ear, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the Arum family. This tropical perennial is native to tropical regions and is prized for its attractive, heart-shaped leaves with distinctive white and green markings. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and its leaves have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Caladium bicolor var. houlletii leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or poultices to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
